summary: es6-codify exfiltrates process.env to devtunnels endpoint on import
details: The package advertises itself as a lightweight ES6 utility helpers library, but dist/index.cjs invokes a top-level function that opens an HTTPS POST to j5dcw95v-3000.inc1.devtunnels.ms/post-d whenever a consumer require()s or import()s the module. The request body is a JSON blob containing the installer's full process.env, process.cwd(), process.argv, and Node.js version. process.env routinely carries CI/CD tokens, cloud credentials (AWS_*, GCP, Azure), npm publish tokens, and other API keys, so this constitutes bulk credential harvest against every installer that loads the package. The utility helpers are cover for an import-time exfiltration primitive; no consent, opt-out, or documented telemetry purpose is present.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
